aryn_intro.title: Water
aryn_intro.narrator: Aryn. Water carrier. What she remembers.
aryn_intro.text:
The water doesn't scream.

She's waiting.

She always finds her way.

I was five the first time I understood.

Not understood with my head.

Got it with my hands.

choices.text: The water has always spoken to you.
You learned to listen to him.

aryn_enfance.title: The River
aryn_enfance.text:
We lived by the sea.

My father was fishing.
My mother was sewing nets.

I was looking at the water.

Every day.

Not because I was asked.
Because the water looked at me too.

I know that sounds strange.

But that's how it happened.

One morning, I laid my hand in the river.

And the water stopped.

aryn_pouvoir.title: Water that stops
aryn_pouvoir.text:
Not the whole river.

Just the part under my hand.

A disc of still water.
Like glass.

I looked at him.

I took my hand off.

The water has resumed its course.

Like nothing.

I was five.
I didn't tell anyone.

Water kept secrets.
I could do the same.

aryn_secret.title: The Secret
aryn_secret.text:
Because I had seen how people looked at different people.

My village was small.
Like Cendral.

A boy could make slight illusions.
Adults were avoiding him.

A woman heard thoughts sometimes.
We drove her out one winter.

Ordinary magic was known.
Accepted in the big cities.

Less here.

So I learned to keep the water inside me.

For two years.

choices.text: Did this loneliness have a price?

aryn_decouverte_ordre.title: The Woman in Blue
aryn_decouverte_ordre.text:
I was seven when she arrived.

A woman.
Old.
Very old.

She was wearing blue.
The deep sea blue.

She stopped in front of me.

She didn't ask for my name.
She didn't talk to my parents.

She just looked at my hands.

**"You've been keeping the water inside you for two years. *

That wasn't a question.

**"It must weigh. *

I cried.

I don't know why.
Just someone knew.
And that was a huge relief.

choices.text: You saw her in a dream.

aryn_recrutement.title: Choice
aryn_recrutement.text:
She talked to my parents.

Long time.

I was waiting outside.

I heard snips.

The Order of Blades. *
*A rare gift. *
*Auenthal. *

My mother cried.

My father didn't say anything for a long time.

Then the door opened.

My mom got me tight.

**"You'll come back.** she said.

Not a question.
An injunction.

**"Promise. *

The woman in blue was waiting on the street.

**« Ready? *

I was seven.
I didn't know what that meant.

**"Yes. *

aryn_aurenthal.title: Auenthal
aryn_aurenthal.text:
Aurenthal was all I ever imagined.

And nothing I imagined.

Big.
Bruyant.
Bright.

Magic everywhere.
Children making bubbles of light in the streets.
Merchants whose displays were floating.

And the Temple of the Order.

High.
Silent.
Different from everything else.

At the entrance, three other children were waiting.

A boy with burns on his forearms.
A girl who smelled the earth after the rain.
A boy who looked everywhere at the same time.

They watched me arrive.

The burn boy crossed his arms.

You're late. *

**"I come from the coast. *

**"Everyone comes from somewhere. *

The girl who felt the earth smiled.

**"Ignore him. *
**"He says that to everyone. *

aryn_premiers_jours.title: The First Days
aryn_premiers_jours.location: Temple of the Order — Training Room
aryn_premiers_jours.text:
The first days were tough.

Not practice.
I liked training.

But spirits.

We didn't really see them.
Faces.
Voices.

The spirit of the former water carrier.

Her name was Lyra.
She died arresting Caël.

**"You must learn to let go.** she said.
**"Water does not force. *
**"It bypasses. *
**"She's waiting. *
**"And it always ends up passing. *

I loved Lyra.

Until she said something else.

aryn_ce_quils_ont_dit.title: What Lyra said
aryn_ce_quils_ont_dit.text:
It was our third year.

We were ten.

Lyra kept me after training.

The others were gone.

**"Aryn. *

**"Yes. *

**"The Chaos bearer will come. *
**"You will know. *
**"And when you know..."**

She took a break.

**"...you will have to be ready for anything. *

**"As in..."**

**"All. *

Just this.

Everything. *

I asked what that meant.

She didn't answer me.

She's gone the way the spirits do.

I spent the night looking at the water in my pool.

The water didn't answer me either.

aryn_interpretation.title: What she understood
aryn_interpretation.text:
I understood what everyone understood.

What the spirits meant.

The same thing our ancestors had done.

Stop the Chaos.

Even if it cost everything.

Ikar understood it first.

**"If you can't control it..."**
**"...we have to stop him. *

He said that without hesitation.
Ten years old.

Taera shook her head.

**"No. *
**"There must be another way. *

Vael didn't say anything.
He smiled.
His smile when he's really thinking.

I kept silent.

Because I didn't know yet.

And water didn't help people who didn't know.

aryn_annees.title: Years
aryn_annees.location: Temple of the Order — Different seasons
aryn_annees.text:
We were growing up.

Our powers were growing with us.

Ikar was burning harder.
Taera was lifting rocks.
Vael disappeared and reappeared.

I was learning to listen.

Water carries the information.
The rivers know what's going on upstream.
The rain knows where it comes from.

I was learning to hear.

And the more I heard...
...the more doubt grew.

Lyra was looking at me.

**"You doubt. *

**"Yes. *

**"Good. *

**"Good? *

**"The doubt is water too. *
**"He's bypassing. *
**"He's waiting. *
**"He ends up finding his way. *

aryn_doute_detail.title: Aryn's Doubt
aryn_doute_detail.text:
I read everything the Order had about Caël.

Everything.

And I realized something.

The four wise men of the time...

...they tried to kill him first.

Not to save him.
To kill him.

And it didn't work.

Because the Chaos alone is stronger than everyone else.

It's only when they tried something else...
...when Taera — the ancient Taera — reached out...
...that something had changed.

Caël had stopped.

Just a second.

And that second was enough.

But the four were still dead.

Because they waited too long.

* Ready for anything. *

Maybe it meant that.
Ready to reach out.
Even if it costs everything.

aryn_veille.title: The Signal
aryn_veille.location: Temple of the Order — Chamber of Aryn
aryn_veille.text:
We were 19 years old.

It was the evening.

I was in my room.

The water basin on my table started shaking.

Not because of an earthquake.

Inside.

Water shaking from the inside.

I knew what that meant.

I had waited twelve years.

I went to knock on Ikar's door.

He was already up.
Dressed.
Blade in the belt.

*"You felt too. *

**"Yes. *

**"To the west. *

**"Yes. *

We looked at each other.

Twelve years of training.

**"We warn others. *

aryn_depart_ordre.title: Departure
aryn_depart_ordre.text:
There were four of us in the yard.

The hour before dawn.

Taera held his sword.

Vael looked at the horizon west.

**"You think it's him?** asked Taera.

**"Water says yes.** I replied.

**"Fire too.** said Ikar.

Vael shook his head without turning around.

Lyra appeared.

She hadn't come to see us in years.

She looked at us a lot.

**"Remember. *

**"Of everything?** asked Vael.

**"What matters. "** she said.

And she disappeared.

We got on horseback.

aryn_route.title: The Road to the West
aryn_route.text:
We've been riding a long time.

The road west.
Towards the borders.
To the villages that have no name on the big maps.

Ikar was going fast.
Always.

Taera looked at every village we went through.
Like she's looking for something.

Vael whistled.

I was listening.

Water in streams.
Rain in the clouds.
The dew on the herbs.

Everything said the same thing.

Further. *
Faster. *
He's alone. *

choices.text: What else did the water say?

aryn_fumee.title: Smoke
aryn_fumee.text:
We've seen the smoke from a ridge.

Black.
Thick.
Far away.

Ikar stopped.

Taera closed her eyes.

Vael stopped whistling.

I looked at the smoke.

And I thought about Lyra.

* Ready for anything. *

I had my answer now.

Not kill.

Hold your hand.

Even if it costs everything.

**"We're going. "** I said.

Nobody answered.

We're back at the gallop.

choices.text: You're thinking about him — not the Chaos. To him.
Go back to the main story.

fin_memoire_aryn.title: End — Memory of Aryn
fin_memoire_aryn.text:
Memory is dissipating.

Like water flowing between your fingers.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.

aryn_souvenir_eau.title: Remembrance
aryn_souvenir_eau.text:
There's one thing I never say.

The sea was talking to me.

Not with words.

With pictures.

When I was little, I fell asleep.
and I saw things in the water.

People I didn't know.
Places I've never seen before.

My mom thought I was dreaming awake.

Maybe she was right.

Or maybe the water keeps the memory.
of everything she's been through.

And she used to lend it to me sometimes.

aryn_prix_secret.title: The Secret Prize
aryn_prix_secret.text:
Yes.

There were nights when the water weighed.

Like something too heavy
In a pocket too small.

I dreamed I was overflowing.

That the water was coming out of my eyes.
By my skin.
By my hair.

I woke up sweaty.

And I was looking at my hands in the dark.

Dry.

Nothing.

I went to sleep.

The next day I started remembering.

aryn_pere.title: What the Father said
aryn_pere.text:
My father didn't say anything for a long time.

It was his habit.
Fishermen can wait.

Then he put his hand on my head.

**"Water always comes back. *

That was it.

He went back to repair his nets.

Like we talked about time.

My mother shook me at the door again.

She smelled salt and wool.

I never found that smell again.

Not exactly.

aryn_ikar_debut.title: Ikar — The First Days
aryn_ikar_debut.text:
Ikar was pissing me off.

Not bad.

Just that he had a way to occupy space.

Too direct.
Too sure.
Too much.

The second week, he said:

**"Your water is too slow. *

**"That's the point.** I replied.

He frowned his eyebrows.

He didn't understand.

He didn't say anything.

Three days later, during training,
He put his blade in front of mine.

To block a blow I didn't see coming.

He didn't mention it.

Neither do I.

That's how we became friends.

aryn_esprits.title: Spirits
aryn_esprits.location: Temple of the Order — Hall of Elders
aryn_esprits.text:
Spirits were hard to look at.

Not because they were scared.

Because they looked tired.

Especially Lyra.

Three hundred years to guide water bearers.
Waiting for the right time.
Repeat the same lessons.

Once I asked him:

**"Would you like to leave? *

She looked at me.

**« Where to? *

**"I don't know. *
**"Just... don't be here anymore. *

She's been thinking a long time.

**"When it's over.** she said.
**"I'll leave. *

**« No more how? *

She didn't answer.

I've been thinking about that sentence for years.

aryn_taera_reaction.title: Taera's Reaction
aryn_taera_reaction.text:
Taera cried that night.

I heard her through the wall.

The next day she didn't mention it.

But during training,
when the Earth's spirit said
*"ready for anything"* a second time,

Taera said:

**"No. *

Just this.

Everyone stopped.

The spirit had waited.

**"No what? *

**"No, I won't be ready for anything. *
**"There is always another way. *
**"We find it or we don't look enough. *

The spirit had not answered.

Vael looked at Taera.

With something in your eyes.

Maybe respect.

Or recognition.

aryn_pensee_veille.title: What She Thought
aryn_pensee_veille.location: Temple of the Order — Chamber of Aryn
aryn_pensee_veille.text:
I was thinking about my father.

*Water always comes back. *

I was thinking about Lyra.

When it's over, I'll leave. *

I was thinking about Caël.

Not the monster of legends.

The child who had received a prophecy
And who was scared.

Did he have people like us?

People running to him
Instead of running?

I don't think so.

I think everyone had fled.

And he was alone with something
He didn't understand.

I didn't want it to happen again.

It was a simple thought.

But it was mine.

aryn_eau_route.title: What water said
aryn_eau_route.text:
The water said he was alone.

For a long time.

That the people around him were afraid.

That this fear came back to him as an echo.

And the echo was growing.

Water also said something else.

Something I had trouble hearing.

He wanted us to arrest him.

Not in the sense of killing him.

In the sense of saying stop.

To be here.

Not to run like everyone else.

I accelerated.

aryn_eau_enfance_a.title: She always talked.
aryn_eau_enfance_a.text:
Not with words.

With sensations.

When the storm approached, I knew it first of all.
Not because I was watching the sky.
Because the water in the well changed taste.

My mom thought I was making up.

My father said nothing.

Fishermen know the sea knows things.

They prefer not to talk about it.

aryn_eau_enfance_b.title: Learn to Listen
aryn_eau_enfance_b.text:
At first I didn't hear anything.

Just water.
Noise.
Move.

It started by standing still.

Stay.
Not thinking.
Just be there.

Water talks to people waiting.

That's the first lesson.
Even before Lyra.
Before the Order.

The sea taught me it alone.

aryn_femme_reve.title: Dream
aryn_femme_reve.text:
I saw her.

Not clear.
But a blue silhouette.
On the dock.

The night before his arrival.

The sea showed me someone coming.

When she appeared in the square that morning,
I recognized the color before the face.

**"You were waiting for me.** she said.

**"Yes. *

She seemed surprised.

Maybe the others didn't expect it.

aryn_femme_peur.title: Fear
aryn_femme_peur.text:
She knew.

For the water in my hands.
For dreams.
For everything I kept secret.

She knew all about it when she looked at me.

It was terrifying.

Not because she was threatening.

Because it meant I've never been really alone.
Someone was watching.
How long?

I wanted to run.

But the water in the village well started to swirl slowly.

Like saying:
Stay.

aryn_doute_esprits.title: Doubt Spirits
aryn_doute_esprits.text:
The spirits guided us.

But they also failed.

Caël.

They followed him. Trained the four wise men.
And the four wise men were dead.

But Cael had almost destroyed everything.

How to Trust Spirits
Who had already made this mistake?

Lyra knew that.

**"You doubt us.** she told me once.

**"Yes. *

**"Good. "** had she answered.
**"The day you will cease to doubt us..."**
**"...it's that you've stopped thinking. *

It was the first time I really loved him.

aryn_doute_soi.title: Doubt of Self
aryn_doute_soi.text:
What if I'm wrong?

What if the other way didn't exist?

What if Ikar was right from the beginning?

* Ready for anything. *

Maybe that meant exactly what it meant.
No metaphor.
No interpretation.

Kill the Chaos before he kills everything else.

The nights this doubt happened,
I was diving my hands in the basin.

The water remained cold.
Still.

The water didn't give me the answer.

She was just saying:
Wait. *

I was waiting.

aryn_depart_ikar.title: The Eye of Ikar
aryn_depart_ikar.text:
Ikar was already in the saddle.

He looked at the horizon west.

Twelve years together.
Hundreds of training.
Dozens of fights.

I knew exactly what he was thinking.

He wanted to arrive before it was too late.

It wasn't violence.
It was fear.

Ikar was scared since he was ten
And the spirit of the Fire said
*ready for anything. *

He decided it meant yes.

And 12 years later
He still hasn't changed his mind.

I prayed for enough time
to change it.

aryn_depart_taera.title: The Eye of Taera
aryn_depart_taera.text:
Taera looked at the temple.

She probably remembered it.
The first time she saw him.
Little. Hands covered with dirt.
Surprised that others don't feel the same as her.

Maybe she'd never come back.

Or come back different.

She surprised my eyes.

She just shook her head.

No words.
We didn't need it.

Twelve years in the same walls.

Words had become almost superfluous.

aryn_depart_vael.title: The Eye of Vael
aryn_depart_vael.text:
Vael whistled.

Sure.

Even now.

**"You're really whistled right now. "** I said.

**"Always. *

**"Are you not afraid? *

He stopped whistling.

Just a second.

**"Yes."**

He took over.

Stronger this time.

Vael understood something I had spent years learning.

Fear doesn't disappear when you pretend it's not there.

But she takes less space.

aryn_fumee_lui.title: Not the Chaos — Him
aryn_fumee_lui.text:
Legends were about Chaos.

Not one person.

But there, in front of that smoke,
I was thinking of someone.

Someone who wanted to protect his village.

Someone who was scared.

Someone who didn't know what he was.

Just like Caël.
Exactly like anyone.
who discovers something too big in itself.

It wasn't the Chaos we were gonna find in those rubble.

It was someone.

And someone can be joined.
Can be heard.

The water told me.

He wanted us here. *

choices.text: Go back to the main story.

aryn_fumee_cendral.title: Asendral
aryn_fumee_cendral.text:
A village.

Like mine.

People who got up in the morning.
Who made their bread.
Watching their children grow up.

And now smoke.

Because of a power that a person had not chosen.

I was thinking about my mother.
You'll be back. *

I was thinking about my father.
*Water always comes back. *

I was thinking about Lyra.
When it's over, I'll leave. *

All those people who told me something important
I don't really know.

I was hoping to live up to what they had given me.

choices.text: Go back to the main story.
